PSA.117.1.definition: to be clear (orig. of sound, but usually of color); to shine; hence, to make a show, to boast; and thus to be (clamorously) foolish; to rave; causativ
properly, self (but generally used to point out more definitely the object of a verb or preposition, even or namely)
(the) self-existent or eternal; jeho-vah, jewish national name of god
properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every (in the singular only, but often in a plural sense)
a foreign nation; hence, a gentile; also (figuratively) a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts
properly, to address in a loud tone, i.e., (specifically) loud
properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every (in the singular only, but often in a plural sense)
a collection, i.e., community of persons

PSA.117.2.definition: (by implication) very widely used as a relative conjunction or adverb (as below); often largely modified by other particles annexed
to be strong; by implication, to prevail, act insolently
above, over, upon, or against (yet always in this last relation with a downward aspect) in a great variety of applications
kindness; by implication (towards god) piety; rarely (by opposition) reproof, or (subject.) beauty
(the) self-existent or eternal; jeho-vah, jewish national name of god
properly, concealed, i.e., the vanishing point; generally, time out of mind (past or future), i.e., (practically) eternity; frequentatively, adverbial
to be clear (orig. of sound, but usually of color); to shine; hence, to make a show, to boast; and thus to be (clamorously) foolish; to rave; causativ
